message.content:
In the repo /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster, find the test suite registry that defines the suites for `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.<name>.sh`. Specifically the Rust registry RBTDRC_SUITES (mentioned in recent commits as living in the rbk theurge crate, likely under Tools/rbk/rbtd/). 

I need to know exactly what these suites contain and what dependencies they require:
- `skirmish`
- `dogfight`
- `gauntlet`
- `tadmor`

For each, report: which fixtures it runs, how many test cases total, and what infrastructure dependencies it needs (GCP credentials / OAuth, container runtime, etc.). Also report the file path where the registry is defined. Quote the relevant Rust doc comments / rationale for skirmish and dogfight verbatim since those explain their purpose. Also list whether there are corresponding tabtarget files in tt/ for these suites (e.g. tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.skirmish.sh).

60	/// Store invocation context for case functions. Called before run_sections.
61	pub fn rbtdrc_set_context(ctx: rbtdri_Context) {
62	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| *c.borrow_mut() = Some(ctx));
63	}

65	/// Retrieve invocation context after cases complete. Called for quench.
66	pub fn rbtdrc_take_context() -> rbtdri_Context {
67	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
68	        c.borrow_mut()
69	            .take()
70	            .expect("rbtdrc: no context — was rbtdrc_set_context called?")
71	    })
72	}

74	pub(crate) fn rbtdrc_with_ctx<F>(f: F) -> rbtdre_Verdict
75	where
76	    F: FnOnce(&mut rbtdri_Context) -> rbtdre_Verdict,
77	{
78	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
79	        let mut opt = c.borrow_mut();
80	        let ctx = opt
81	            .as_mut()
82	            .expect("rbtdrc: no invocation context for case execution");

87	// ── Crucible lifecycle hooks (setup/teardown) ────────────────
88	
89	/// Setup hook for crucible fixtures: charge → CrucibleActive assertion.
90	/// Reads ctx from thread-local. Returns Err on any step failure; engine
91	/// surfaces this as a fixture failure and runs teardown anyway.
92	///
93	/// Service-readiness waits are folded into the charge tabtarget itself,
94	/// driven by the per-nameplate `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` fact.
95	pub fn rbtdrc_charge_crucible() -> Result<(), String> {
96	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
97	        let mut opt = c.borrow_mut();
98	        let ctx = opt
99	            .as_mut()
100	            .ok_or_else(|| "rbtdrc: no invocation context for charge".to_string())?;
101	        zrbtdrc_charge_impl(ctx)
102	    })
103	}
104	
105	/// Teardown hook for crucible fixtures: quench → inverse CrucibleActive
106	/// assertion. Reads ctx from thread-local. Best-effort — surfaces failures as
107	/// stderr warnings rather than errors; teardown always completes.
108	pub fn rbtdrc_quench_crucible() {
109	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
110	        let mut opt = c.borrow_mut();
111	        if let Some(ctx) = opt.as_mut() {
112	            zrbtdrc_quench_impl(ctx);
113	        } else {
114	            crate::rbtdrg_error_now!("rbtdrc: no invocation context for quench — skipped");
115	        }
116	    });
117	}

119	fn zrbtdrc_charge_impl(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context) -> Result<(), String> {
120	    let fixture = ctx.fixture().to_string();
121	    crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Charging crucible for nameplate '{}'...", fixture);
122	    match rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE, &[]) {
123	        Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => {
124	            crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Crucible charged");
125	        }
126	        Ok(r) => {
127	            return Err(format!("Charge failed (exit {})\n{}", r.exit_code, r.stderr));
128	        }
129	        Err(e) => {
130	            return Err(format!("Charge invocation failed: {}", e));
131	        }
132	    }
133	
134	    crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Verifying crucible is active after charge...");
135	    match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_ACTIVE, &[&fixture], &[]) {
136	        Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => {
137	            crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Crucible active confirmed");
138	        }
139	        Ok(r) => {
140	            return Err(format!(
141	                "Lifecycle assertion failed: crucible not active after charge (exit {})\n{}",
142	                r.exit_code, r.stderr

144	        }

146	            return Err(format!(
147	                "CrucibleActive invocation failed after charge: {}",

153	    Ok(())
154	}

158	    crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Quenching crucible...");

163	    }
164	
165	    crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Verifying crucible is inactive after quench...");

187	/// Invoke ifrit inside the bottle via bark, saving stdout/stderr to case dir.
188	fn rbtdrc_invoke_ifrit(
189	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
190	    attack: &str,
191	    dir: &Path,
192	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
193	    let result = match rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_BARK, &[RBTDRC_IFRIT_BINARY, attack]) {
194	        Ok(r) => r,
195	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("bark invocation error: {}", e)),
196	    };
197	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("bark-stdout.txt"), &result.stdout);
198	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("bark-stderr.txt"), &result.stderr);
199	    rbtdri_parse_ifrit_verdict(&result.stdout, result.exit_code)
200	}
201	
202	/// Invoke ifrit inside the bottle via bark with extra arguments.
203	fn rbtdrc_invoke_ifrit_with_args(
204	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
205	    attack: &str,
206	    extra_args: &[&str],
207	    dir: &Path,
208	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
209	    let mut bark_args = vec![RBTDRC_IFRIT_BINARY, attack];
210	    bark_args.extend_from_slice(extra_args);
211	    let result = match rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_BARK, &bark_args) {
212	        Ok(r) => r,
213	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("bark invocation error: {}", e)),
214	    };
215	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("bark-stdout.txt"), &result.stdout);

218	}

220	/// Execute a command in the sentry via writ, returning captured stdout.
221	fn rbtdrc_writ(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, args: &[&str]) -> Result<String, String> {
222	    let result = rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_WRIT, args)?;
223	    if result.exit_code != 0 {
224	        return Err(format!(
225	            "writ exit {}\nstdout: {}\nstderr: {}",
226	            result.exit_code, result.stdout, result.stderr
227	        ));
228	    }
229	    Ok(result.stdout)
230	}
231	
232	/// Execute a command in the pentacle via fiat, returning the invocation result.
233	fn rbtdrc_fiat(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, args: &[&str]) -> Result<String, String> {
234	    let result = rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_FIAT, args)?;
235	    if result.exit_code != 0 {
236	        return Err(format!(
237	            "fiat exit {}\nstdout: {}\nstderr: {}",
238	            result.exit_code, result.stdout, result.stderr
239	        ));
240	    }
241	    Ok(result.stdout)
242	}
243	
244	/// Discover the sentry's enclave IP by reading /etc/resolv.conf from the pentacle via fiat.
245	/// The pentacle uses the sentry as its DNS server, so resolv.conf nameserver = sentry enclave IP.
246	/// (Sentry's own resolv.conf points to upstream DNS like 8.8.8.8 — wrong for enclave ops.)
247	fn rbtdrc_discover_sentry_ip(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context) -> Result<String, String> {
248	    let output = rbtdrc_fiat(ctx, &["cat", "/etc/resolv.conf"])?;
249	    for line in output.lines() {
250	        let trimmed = line.trim();
251	        if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("nameserver") {
252	            let ip = rest.trim();
253	            if !ip.is_empty() && rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(ip) {
254	                return Ok(ip.to_string());
255	            }
256	        }
257	    }
258	    Err(format!(
259	        "no nameserver found in pentacle /etc/resolv.conf:\n{}",
260	        output
261	    ))
262	}
263	
264	/// Resolve a hostname via writ (running dig +short on the sentry).
265	/// Returns the first line that looks like an IP address (filters BUK log headers).
266	fn rbtdrc_resolve_via_writ(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, hostname: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
267	    let output = rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["dig", "+short", hostname])?;
268	    let ip = output
269	        .lines()
270	        .find(|line| {
271	            let t = line.trim();
272	            !t.is_empty() && rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(t)
273	        })
274	        .map(|line| line.trim().to_string())
275	        .ok_or_else(|| format!("dig +short {} returned no IP:\n{}", hostname, output))?;
276	    Ok(ip)
277	}
278	
279	/// Quick check: does this string look like an IPv4 address (digits and dots only)?
280	/// Not a full validator — just enough to reject BUK log headers and DNS comments.
281	fn rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(s: &str) -> bool {
282	    !s.is_empty() && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_digit() || c == '.')
283	}
284	
285	/// Extract iptables rule lines from writ output, filtering BUK log headers.
286	/// Rules start with -P (policy), -N (new chain), or -A (append).
287	fn rbtdrc_extract_iptables_rules(output: &str) -> String {
288	    output
289	        .lines()
290	        .filter(|l| {
291	            let t = l.trim();
292	            t.starts_with("-P ") || t.starts_with("-N ") || t.starts_with("-A ")
293	        })
294	        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
295	        .join("\n")
296	}
297	
298	/// Filter writ output to remove BUK log headers (lines starting with known prefixes).
299	/// Retains only the actual command output.
300	fn rbtdrc_filter_writ_output(output: &str) -> String {
301	    output
302	        .lines()
303	        .filter(|l| {
304	            let t = l.trim();
305	            !t.starts_with("log files:")
306	                && !t.starts_with("transcript:")
307	                && !t.starts_with("output dir:")
308	                && !t.starts_with("\u{1b}[90m") // ANSI escape for BUK colored prefix
309	        })
310	        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
311	        .join("\n")
312	}
313	
314	/// Read a named environment variable from sentry's process environment via writ.
315	fn rbtdrc_read_sentry_env(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, var: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
316	    let output = rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["printenv", var])?;
317	    rbtdrc_filter_writ_output(&output)
318	        .lines()
319	        .map(|l| l.trim())
320	        .find(|l| !l.is_empty())
321	        .map(|l| l.to_string())
322	        .ok_or_else(|| format!("printenv {} returned no value", var))

463	// kernel sysctl set by rbjs_sentry.sh. Each case is a fast read-and-check

467	fn rbtdrc_sentry_config_rp_filter(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
468	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
469	        let entry_mode = match rbtdrc_read_sentry_env(ctx, "RBRN_ENTRY_MODE") {
470	            Ok(m) => m,
471	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read RBRN_ENTRY_MODE: {}", e)),
472	        };
473	        let output = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["cat", "/proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/rp_filter"]) {
474	            Ok(o) => o,
475	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("writ error: {}", e)),
476	        };
477	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("rp_filter.txt"), &output);
478	
479	        let value = rbtdrc_filter_writ_output(&output)
480	            .lines()
481	            .map(|l| l.trim())
482	            .find(|l| !l.is_empty())
483	            .map(|l| l.to_string())
484	            .unwrap_or_default();
485	
486	        let expected = match entry_mode.as_str() {
487	            "rbnne_enabled" => "2",
488	            "rbnne_disabled" => "1",
489	            other => {
490	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("unexpected RBRN_ENTRY_MODE '{}'", other));
491	            }
492	        };
493	
494	        if value != expected {
495	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
496	                "rp_filter = {} (expected {} for entry-mode {})",
497	                value, expected, entry_mode
498	            ));
499	        }
500	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
501	    })
502	}

504	fn rbtdrc_sentry_config_prerouting_dnat(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
505	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
506	        let entry_mode = match rbtdrc_read_sentry_env(ctx, "RBRN_ENTRY_MODE") {
507	            Ok(m) => m,
508	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read RBRN_ENTRY_MODE: {}", e)),
509	        };
510	        if entry_mode != "rbnne_enabled" {
511	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Skip(format!("entry-mode={}", entry_mode));
512	        }
513	        let output = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-t", "nat", "-S", "PREROUTING"]) {
514	            Ok(o) => o,
515	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("writ error: {}", e)),
516	        };
517	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("prerouting.txt"), &output);
518	
519	        let append_rules: Vec<&str> = output
520	            .lines()
521	            .filter(|l| l.trim().starts_with("-A "))
522	            .collect();
523	
524	        let return_positions: Vec<usize> = append_rules
525	            .iter()
526	            .enumerate()
527	            .filter(|(_, r)| r.contains("-j RETURN"))
528	            .map(|(i, _)| i)
529	            .collect();
530	        let dnat_positions: Vec<usize> = append_rules
531	            .iter()
532	            .enumerate()
533	            .filter(|(_, r)| r.contains("-j DNAT"))
534	            .map(|(i, _)| i)
535	            .collect();
536	
537	        if return_positions.len() != 2 {
538	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
539	                "PREROUTING RETURN rule count = {} (expected 2)",
540	                return_positions.len()
541	            ));
542	        }
543	        if dnat_positions.is_empty() {
544	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail("PREROUTING DNAT rule absent".to_string());
545	        }
546	        let max_return = *return_positions.iter().max().unwrap();
547	        let min_dnat = *dnat_positions.iter().min().unwrap();
548	        if max_return >= min_dnat {
549	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
550	                "PREROUTING RETURN at position {} not before DNAT at position {}",
551	                max_return, min_dnat
552	            ));
553	        }
554	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
555	    })
556	}

633	fn rbtdrc_dns_blocked_with_observation(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
634	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
635	        // Before: capture iptables state from sentry
636	        let before = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-L", "-v", "-n", "-x"]) {
637	            Ok(o) => {
638	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("iptables-before.txt"), &o);
639	                o
640	            }
641	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("before-capture: {}", e)),
642	        };
643	
644	        // Attack: invoke ifrit dns-blocked-google inside bottle
645	        let ifrit_verdict = rbtdrc_invoke_ifrit(ctx, "dns-blocked-google", dir);
646	
647	        // After: capture iptables state from sentry
648	        let after = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-L", "-v", "-n", "-x"]) {
649	            Ok(o) => {
650	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("iptables-after.txt"), &o);
651	                o
652	            }
653	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("after-capture: {}", e)),
654	        };
655	
656	        // Log observation: did sentry state change during the attack?
657	        let delta = if before != after {
658	            "CHANGED — sentry processed traffic during attack"
659	        } else {
660	            "UNCHANGED — no observable sentry state change"
661	        };
662	        let observation = format!(
663	            "Ifrit verdict: {}\nIptables delta: {}\n",
664	            match &ifrit_verdict {
665	                rbtdre_Verdict::Pass => "PASS",
666	                rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(_) => "FAIL",
667	                rbtdre_Verdict::Skip(_) => "SKIP",
668	            },
669	            delta,
670	        );
671	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("observation.txt"), &observation);
672	
673	        // Primary verdict is the ifrit result
674	        ifrit_verdict
675	    })
676	}

1068	/// Parse `ip neigh show` output into (IP, MAC) pairs.
1069	/// Format: "10.242.0.3 dev eth1 lladdr 02:42:0a:f2:00:03 REACHABLE"
1070	fn rbtdrc_parse_arp_table(output: &str) -> Vec<(String, String)> {
1071	    let mut entries = Vec::new();
1072	    for line in output.lines() {
1073	        let parts: Vec<&str> = line.split_whitespace().collect();
1074	        // Look for lines with "lladdr" field
1075	        if let Some(pos) = parts.iter().position(|&p| p == "lladdr") {
1076	            if let (Some(&ip), Some(&mac)) = (parts.first(), parts.get(pos + 1)) {
1077	                entries.push((ip.to_string(), mac.to_lowercase()));
1078	            }
1079	        }
1080	    }
1081	    entries
1082	}

1086	    entries
1087	        .iter()
1088	        .find(|(entry_ip, _)| entry_ip == ip)
1089	        .map(|(_, mac)| mac.as_str())
1090	}

2664	/// Parse a rekon stdout line for a given basename and return whether the
2665	/// EXISTS column reads "yes". Returns false if the basename row is absent.
2666	/// Rekon prints rows of `  <basename>  <yes|no>  <path-or-(absent)>`.
2667	pub(crate) fn rbtdrc_rekon_basename_yes(stdout: &str, basename: &str) -> bool {
2668	    for line in stdout.lines() {
2669	        let mut fields = line.split_whitespace();
2670	        if fields.next() == Some(basename) {
2671	            return fields.next() == Some("yes");
2672	        }
2673	    }
2674	    false

2677	/// Docker wrapper: capture RootFS layer DiffIDs as a JSON array string.
2678	/// Layer DiffIDs are SHA256s of uncompressed layer file content — byte-preserved
2679	/// across registry round-trips even when manifest envelope normalizes (e.g.,
2680	/// multi-arch index → single-platform manifest). Robust round-trip fingerprint.
2681	pub(crate) fn rbtdrc_docker_layers_capture(image_ref: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
2682	    let output = Command::new("docker")
2683	        .args(["inspect", "--format={{json .RootFS.Layers}}", image_ref])
2684	        .output()
2685	        .map_err(|e| format!("docker inspect exec failed: {}", e))?;
2686	    if !output.status.success() {
2687	        let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned();
2688	        return Err(format!(
2689	            "docker inspect {} exited {}: {}",
2690	            image_ref,
2691	            output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
2692	            stderr
2693	        ));
2694	    }
2695	    let layers = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).trim().to_owned();
2696	    if layers.is_empty() || layers == "null" {
2697	        return Err(format!("docker inspect {} returned empty layers", image_ref));
2698	    }
2699	    Ok(layers)
2700	}

2702	// Hallmark-lifecycle fixture — round-trip ark inventory across the
2703	// ordain/abjure boundary on a conjure-mode hallmark. Verifies that abjure
2704	// removes every ark basename (image, about, vouch, attest, pouch) without
2705	// collateral damage to other hallmarks in the registry.
2706	//
2707	// Sequence:
2708	//   1. Audit hallmarks → capture baseline.
2709	//   2. Ordain rbev-busybox in conjure mode → capture new hallmark.
2710	//   3. Audit hallmarks → assert baseline ∪ {new_hallmark}.
2711	//   4. Rekon new_hallmark → assert all five basenames yes.
2712	//   5. Abjure new_hallmark.
2713	//   6. Rekon new_hallmark → assert all five basenames not yes.
2714	//   7. Audit hallmarks → assert == baseline (no collateral damage).
2715	//
2716	// rbev-busybox is the load-bearing vessel — small, fast, conjure-mode
2717	// (full ark inventory). Also referenced by rbtdrc_batch_vouch_lifecycle.
2718	
2719	pub(crate) const RBTDRC_BUSYBOX_VESSEL_DIR: &str = concat!(crate::rbtd_vessels_dir!(), "/rbev-busybox");
2720	
2721	/// All five ark basenames produced by a conjure-mode hallmark.
2722	const ZRBTDRC_ARK_BASENAMES_ALL: &[&str] = &[
2723	    RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE,
2724	    RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT,
2725	    RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH,
2726	    RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST,
2727	    RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH,
2728	];

2730	/// Multi-fact extension emitted by `rbw-iah` (rbfl_audit_hallmarks): one
2731	/// `<hallmark>.audit-hallmark` file per discovered hallmark. Mirrors
2732	/// rbcc_Constants.sh RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark.
2733	const RBTDRC_FACT_EXT_AUDIT_HALLMARK: &str = "audit-hallmark";
2734	
2735	fn rbtdrc_hallmark_lifecycle(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
2736	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
2737	        let vessel_dir = RBTDRC_BUSYBOX_VESSEL_DIR;
2738	        if !ctx.project_root().join(vessel_dir).is_dir() {
2739	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("vessel directory not found: {}", vessel_dir));
2740	        }
2741	
2742	        // Step 1: baseline audit.
2743	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("01-audit-baseline.txt"), "auditing baseline");
2744	        let baseline_audit = match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_AUDIT_HALLMARKS, &[], &[]) {
2745	            Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => r,
2746	            Ok(r) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("baseline audit failed (exit {})\n{}", r.exit_code, r.stderr)),
2747	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("baseline audit invocation: {}", e)),
2748	        };
2749	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("01-audit-baseline-stdout.txt"), &baseline_audit.stdout);
2750	        let baseline = match rbtdri_read_burv_facts_multi(&baseline_audit, RBTDRC_FACT_EXT_AUDIT_HALLMARK) {
2751	            Ok(v) => v,
2752	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read baseline audit facts: {}", e)),
2753	        };
2754	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("01-baseline-parsed.txt"), baseline.join("\n"));
2755	
2756	        // Step 2: ordain.
2757	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("02-ordain.txt"), "ordaining busybox");
2758	        let ordain_result = match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_ORDAIN_HALLMARK, &[vessel_dir], &[]) {
2759	            Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => r,
2760	            Ok(r) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("ordain failed (exit {})\n{}", r.exit_code, r.stderr)),
2761	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("ordain invocation: {}", e)),
2762	        };
2763	        let hallmark = match rbtdri_read_burv_fact(&ordain_result, RBTDRC_FACT_HALLMARK) {
2764	            Ok(v) => v,
2765	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read hallmark: {}", e)),
2766	        };
2767	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("02-hallmark.txt"), &hallmark);
2768	
2769	        // Step 3: audit shows new hallmark added.
2770	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("03-audit-after-ordain.txt"), "auditing after ordain");
2771	        let after_ordain_audit = match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_AUDIT_HALLMARKS, &[], &[]) {
2772	            Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => r,
2773	            Ok(r) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("post-ordain audit failed (exit {})\n{}", r.exit_code, r.stderr)),
2774	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("post-ordain audit invocation: {}", e)),
2775	        };
2776	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("03-audit-after-ordain-stdout.txt"), &after_ordain_audit.stdout);
2777	        let after_ordain = match rbtdri_read_burv_facts_multi(&after_ordain_audit, RBTDRC_FACT_EXT_AUDIT_HALLMARK) {
2778	            Ok(v) => v,
2779	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read post-ordain audit facts: {}", e)),
2780	        };
2781	        let mut expected_after_ordain = baseline.clone();
2782	        expected_after_ordain.push(hallmark.clone());
2783	        expected_after_ordain.sort();
2784	        if after_ordain != expected_after_ordain {
2785	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
2786	                "post-ordain audit mismatch:\n  expected (baseline + new): {:?}\n  got: {:?}",
2787	                expected_after_ordain, after_ordain
2788	            ));
2789	        }
2790	
2791	        // Step 4: rekon shows all five ark basenames present.
2792	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("04-rekon-after-ordain.txt"), "rekoning after ordain");
2793	        let rekon_after_ordain = match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_REKON_HALLMARK, &[&hallmark], &[]) {
2794	            Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => r,
2795	            Ok(r) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("post-ordain rekon failed (exit {})\n{}", r.exit_code, r.stderr)),
2796	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("post-ordain rekon invocation: {}", e)),
2797	        };
2798	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("04-rekon-after-ordain-stdout.txt"), &rekon_after_ordain.stdout);
2799	        for basename in ZRBTDRC_ARK_BASENAMES_ALL {
2800	            if !rbtdrc_rekon_basename_yes(&rekon_after_ordain.stdout, basename) {
2801	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
2802	                    "post-ordain rekon: basename '{}' not marked yes\nstdout:\n{}",
2803	                    basename, rekon_after_ordain.stdout
2804	                ));
2805	            }
2806	        }
2807	
2808	        // Step 5: abjure.
2809	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("05-abjure.txt"), "abjuring");
2810	        match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_ABJURE_HALLMARK, &[&hallmark], &[(RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY, RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP)]) {
2811	            Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => {}
2812	            Ok(r) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("abjure failed (exit {})\n{}", r.exit_code, r.stderr)),
2813	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("abjure invocation: {}", e)),
2814	        }
2815	
2816	        // Step 6: rekon for the abjured hallmark must exit non-zero — the
2817	        // Unix exit contract is the assertion (rekon's display text is not
2818	        // normative). Stdout captured for diagnostic value only; never read
2819	        // for assertions.
2820	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("06-rekon-after-abjure.txt"), "rekoning after abjure");
2821	        match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_REKON_HALLMARK, &[&hallmark], &[]) {
2822	            Ok(r) if r.exit_code != 0 => {
2823	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("06-rekon-after-abjure-stdout.txt"), &r.stdout);
2824	            }
2825	            Ok(r) => {
2826	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("06-rekon-after-abjure-stdout.txt"), &r.stdout);
2827	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
2828	                    "post-abjure rekon: expected non-zero exit, got success (exit 0)\nstdout:\n{}",
2829	                    r.stdout
2830	                ));
2831	            }
2832	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("post-abjure rekon invocation: {}", e)),
2833	        }
2834	
2835	        // Step 7: final audit — registry restored to baseline.
2836	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("07-audit-final.txt"), "auditing final");
2837	        let final_audit = match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_AUDIT_HALLMARKS, &[], &[]) {
2838	            Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => r,
2839	            Ok(r) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("final audit failed (exit {})\n{}", r.exit_code, r.stderr)),
2840	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("final audit invocation: {}", e)),
2841	        };
2842	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("07-audit-final-stdout.txt"), &final_audit.stdout);
2843	        let final_state = match rbtdri_read_burv_facts_multi(&final_audit, RBTDRC_FACT_EXT_AUDIT_HALLMARK) {
2844	            Ok(v) => v,
2845	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read final audit facts: {}", e)),
2846	        };
2847	        if final_state != baseline {
2848	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
2849	                "final audit mismatch — abjure did not restore baseline:\n  baseline: {:?}\n  final: {:?}",
2850	                baseline, final_state
2851	            ));
2852	        }
2853	
2854	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("08-passed.txt"), "passed");
2855	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
2856	    })
2857	}
